title: toddalia angolensis
intro: Toddalia angolensis, also known as the West African bitter plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Toddalia angolensis include the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-diarrheal agent. Toddalia angolensis can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
